page: UpCloud HIMEM-2xCPU-8GB is a KVM-based virtual server belonging to the High Memory family, configured with 2 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of system memory. The hardware is powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. This configuration features 2 physical cores with a shared CPU allocation model and 1.0 thread per core. Cache specifications include 64 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 16 MB of L3 cache. The server includes a bundled 100 GB SSD for local storage and provides one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks indicate that this instance is positioned in the lower tier for compute-heavy workloads. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance tests via stress-ng place the server in the bottom 10%. Similarly, memory bandwidth benchmarks for both small and large blocks fall into the bottom 25%, with read-only large block speeds at 27.29 GB/sec and write speeds at 16.23 GB/sec. However, single-threaded operations show moderate capability, with gzip compression and bzip3 decompression scoring in the middle 50%. Geekbench results reflect a similar trend, showing average single-core performance but weak multi-core and software compilation capabilities.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the shared CPU allocation and modest resource footprint make this server a budget-friendly option for workloads that do not require dedicated, continuous CPU performance. The 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio provides a balanced memory allocation for lightweight applications. Given the weak database and Redis performance benchmarks, which rank in the bottom 25%, this instance is best suited for basic web serving, development environments, lightweight background tasks, or small-scale general-purpose workloads rather than high-throughput database or intensive data-processing applications.
description: UpCloud HIMEM-2xCPU-8GB is a KVM-based virtual server from the High Memory family, equipped with 2 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features a shared CPU allocation model and includes 100 GB of local SSD storage. Benchmark data shows weak to poor multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance, though single-threaded tasks like gzip compression perform at an average tier. With its shared resource model, this server offers a cost-effective option for workloads with low CPU utilization. It is best suited for general-purpose tasks, lightweight web serving, and development environments that do not require sustained high-performance compute or memory throughput.

tagline: A budget-friendly shared-CPU virtual server with balanced memory and local SSD storage for lightweight general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Features 2 vCPUs and 8.0 GB RAM on an AMD EPYC 7542 processor
Includes 100 GB of bundled local SSD storage
Utilizes a shared CPU allocation model under KVM virtualization
Delivers average single-threaded compression and decompression performance
Best suited for lightweight web serving and development environments
